ddls:
CREATE TABLE "tickets" (
  "ticket_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ticket */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer who submitted the ticket */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product related to the ticket */,
  "service_level_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the service level requested by the customer */,
  "service_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of service requested */,
  "support_section" TEXT /* Section responsible for handling the ticket */,
  "workgroup" TEXT /* Workgroup assigned to handle the ticket */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the ticket was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the ticket was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the ticket */,
  "priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the ticket */,
  "expected_resolution_date" TEXT /* Expected date for resolution */,
  "actual_resolution_date" TEXT /* Actual date when the ticket was resolved */,
  "parent_ticket_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent ticket (if linked) */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes on how the issue was resolved */,
  "escalation_level" INTEGER /* Level of escalation if the ticket is escalated */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the ticket (e.g., phone, email, web) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ticket_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tickets_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tickets_parent_ticket_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_ticket_id") REFERENCES tickets ("ticket_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tickets_service_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_type_id") REFERENCES service_types ("service_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tickets_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tickets_service_level_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_level_id") REFERENCES service_levels ("service_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Customer's address */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Customer's phone number */,
  "company" TEXT /* Customer's company name */,
  "preferred_language" TEXT /* Customer's preferred language for communication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "product_category" TEXT /* Category of the product */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Name of the product manufacturer */,
  "model" TEXT /* Product model */,
  "warranty_expiration_date" TEXT /* Warranty expiration date of the product */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Product serial number */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_levels" (
  "service_level_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service level */,
  "level_name" TEXT /* Name of the service level */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service level */,
  "response_time" TEXT /* Expected response time for the service level */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Expected resolution time for the service level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_level_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_types" (
  "service_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the service type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service type */,
  "severity_level" INTEGER /* Severity level of the service type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ticket_activities" (
  "activity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each ticket activity */,
  "ticket_id" INTEGER /* ID of the ticket the activity is related to */,
  "activity_name" TEXT /* Name of the activity performed */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the activity was performed */,
  "performed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the activity */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of the activity */,
  "activity_details" TEXT /* Detailed information about the activity performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("activity_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ticket_activities_ticket_id FOREIGN KEY ("ticket_id") REFERENCES tickets ("ticket_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ticket_activities_performed_by FOREIGN KEY ("performed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in handling tickets */,
  "section" TEXT /* Section the user belongs to */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "access_level" INTEGER /* Access level of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
